title: michelia ceylanica
intro: Michelia ceylanica, also known as the Ceylon white bodhi or Ceylonese whitebodhi, is a flowering evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka. The plant's flowers, leaves, and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Michelia ceylanica are said to include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Michelia ceylanica can be found in various forms, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness the plant's medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
